source_paper_title: Near-Optimal Performance of Stochastic Model Predictive Control
context:
Section 2.2, Remark 5 (page 7); reiterated in Section 4, Conclusions and Future Work (page 13)

Nevertheless, the dynamic regret analysis regarding the distributional robustness property remains an open question.

Analyzing dynamic regret under inexact knowledge of the distribution presents an intriguing avenue for future research.
title: Dynamic-regret guarantees for SMPC under distributional uncertainty (unknown disturbance distribution)
latex:
Given integers \(T\ge 1\) and \(W\ge 0\), consider the discrete-time stochastic control system
\[
 x_t = A(\xi_t)x_{t-1}+B(\xi_t)u_{t-1}+d(\xi_t),\qquad t=0,1,\dots,T,
\]
with state \(x_t\in\mathbb{R}^{n_x}\), control \(u_t\in\mathbb{R}^{n_u}\), and random variable \(\xi_t\in\Xi\). The stage cost is
\[
\ell(x_t,u_t;\xi_t)=\tfrac12\big(x_t^\top Q(\xi_t)x_t + u_t^\top R(\xi_t)u_t\big) - q(\xi_t)^\top x_t - r(\xi_t)^\top u_t.
\]
A (nonanticipative) policy \(\pi\) is a sequence of measurable maps \(u_t=\pi_t(\xi_{0:t})\). For a probability distribution \(\mathbb{P}\) on \(\xi_{0:T}\), define the expected finite-horizon performance
\[
J^{\pi}(\mathbb{P};\xi_0,w_{-1}) := \mathbb{E}_{\xi\sim\mathbb{P}}\Big[\sum_{t=0}^T \ell(x_t,u_t;\xi_t)\,\Big|\,\xi_0\Big],
\]
where \(w_{-1}:=(x_{-1},u_{-1})\) is given and \(x_0=f(w_{-1};\xi_0)\). Let \(J^{\star}(\mathbb{P};\xi_0,w_{-1}) := \inf_{\pi} J^{\pi}(\mathbb{P};\xi_0,w_{-1})\) be the optimal value under \(\mathbb{P}\).

Fix a nominal distribution \(\mathbb{P}_0\) for \(\xi_{0:T}\) and a radius \(\varepsilon>0\). Let \(\mathcal{P}(\varepsilon)\) be an ambiguity set of distributions around \(\mathbb{P}_0\), e.g., a Wasserstein ball
\[
\mathcal{P}(\varepsilon):=\{\mathbb{P}:\mathsf{W}(\mathbb{P},\mathbb{P}_0)\le \varepsilon\},
\]
where \(\mathsf{W}(\cdot,\cdot)\) is a chosen Wasserstein distance on \(\Xi^{T+1}\).

Define the stochastic model predictive control (SMPC) policy \(\pi^{\mathrm{smpc}}_W\) of prediction horizon \(W\) as the receding-horizon policy that, at each time \(\tau\), solves the \(W\)-step conditional stochastic control problem using the nominal conditional distribution induced by \(\mathbb{P}_0\), and applies only the first control action.

Problem: Establish nonasymptotic bounds on the dynamic regret of \(\pi^{\mathrm{smpc}}_W\) under distributional mismatch, namely derive explicit functions \(g\) such that for all \(\mathbb{P}\in\mathcal{P}(\varepsilon)\),
\[
J^{\pi^{\mathrm{smpc}}_W}(\mathbb{P};\xi_0,w_{-1})-J^{\star}(\mathbb{P};\xi_0,w_{-1}) \le g(\varepsilon,W,T,\|w_{-1}\|),
\]
under verifiable structural assumptions (e.g., stabilizability/detectability-type conditions and boundedness/positive definiteness of \(Q(\xi_t),R(\xi_t)\)). In particular, determine whether one can obtain exponential decay in \(W\) (e.g., \(\propto \rho^W\) for some \(\rho\in(0,1)\)) with an additional dependence on \(\varepsilon\), and characterize the best achievable dependence of \(g\) on \(\varepsilon\) and \(T\).
location_in_paper: Section 2.2, Remark 5 (page 7); reiterated in Section 4, Conclusions and Future Work (page 13)
open_problem_quote:
Nevertheless, the dynamic regret analysis regarding the distributional robustness property remains an open question.

Analyzing dynamic regret under inexact knowledge of the distribution presents an intriguing avenue for future research.
subject_classification: Stochastic Optimal Control / Distributionally Robust Optimization
